---
title: "Synthetic Control Slides"
subtitle: "Placeholder deck for the SCM sequence"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [white, workshop-theme.scss]
    slide-level: 2
    slide-number: false
    progress: true
    controls: true
    overview: true
    transition: fade
    center: false
    width: 1400
    height: 850
    footer: '<a href="../index.html">Back to site</a>'
    preview-links: auto
execute:
  echo: false
  eval: false
---

## Deck Status

::: {.hero-card .placeholder-card}
<span class="hero-tag">Placeholder</span>

### Synthetic Control Sequence

This deck slot is reserved for the synthetic control concept sequence. It will become the short entry-point deck for donor-pool logic, counterfactual construction, diagnostics, and practical use.

- next source material: [Synthetic Control notes](../notes/scm/synthetic-control.qmd)
- next worked example: [Synthetic Control Design Lab](../labs/synthetic-control-design-lab.qmd)
- next extension: [Synthetic Control Donor Pool Lab](../labs/synthetic-control-donor-pool-lab.qmd)
:::

## Planned Shape

- start with the counterfactual construction problem
- explain why donor-pool choice is the design bottleneck
- show how pre-treatment fit disciplines the comparison
- make the limits explicit: spillovers, anticipation, poor support, and overfitting
